name: Ancient Egyptian
notes: STRUCTURAL ANCESTRY NODE (not a modelled G2P target). Ancient Egyptian is the Afroasiatic language of pharaonic Egypt and the direct ancestor of Coptic; its latest stage, Demotic, precedes Coptic. It exists here only so the ancestry / phylogenetic-distance metrics of the modern varieties that reference it have a node to resolve to; NO phonology is claimed or reconstructed and the minimal grapheme set is a placeholder (afa.json / trk.json stub pattern).

sources.author: Loprieno, A.
sources.title: Ancient Egyptian: A Linguistic Introduction
sources.notes: Reference for Egyptian and its descent to Coptic (ancestry node only).
